data.title: Macquarie Starts Yunnan Aluminium at Outperform with 18.40 Yuan Price Target
Orsted Expects Earnings to Rise This Year as It Works to Shore Up Balance Sheet
data.description:
(07:04 GMT) *Orsted Sees 2025 EBITDA Excluding New Partnership Agreements and Cancellation Fees at DKK25B-DKK28B
(07:04 GMT) *Orsted Maintains Target to Reinstate Dividends From Financial Year 2026
(07:05 GMT) *Orsted: Partnership and Divestment Program is On Track
(07:05 GMT) *Orsted: 2025 Gross Investments Expected to be DKK50B-DKK54B
(07:08 GMT) *Orsted 4Q Rev DKK21.08B
(07:08 GMT) *Orsted: Analysts Saw 4Q Revenue At DKK26.02B
(07:08 GMT) *Orsted 4Q EBITDA DKK8.35B
(07:08 GMT) *Orsted: Analysts Saw 4Q Ebitda At DKK8.65B
(07:09 GMT) *Orsted 4Q Net Loss DKK6.08B
(07:09 GMT) *Orsted: Analysts Saw 4Q Net Profit At DKK2.74B
(07:13 GMT) *Orsted Sees Higher Offshore and Onshore Earnings in 2025
(07:15 GMT) *Orsted Sees Significantly Higher Bioenergy & Other Earnings in 2025
(07:15 GMT) *Orsted: 2025 Gross Investments Seen at DKK50B-DKK54B
(07:58 GMT) Orsted Expects Earnings to Rise This Year as It Works to Shore Up Balance Sheet

By Dominic Chopping

Orsted guided for slightly higher earnings this year as some wind and solar farms ramp up and it expects to receive higher payments from wind-farm contracts to account for inflation.

The Danish renewable-energy company expects earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization excluding new partnerships and cancellation fees at between 25 billion and 28 billion kroner ($3.49 billion-$3.9 billion), up from 24.75 billion kroner in 2024.

The company announced late Wednesday that it will cut its investment program by 25% in the period to 2030 to ensure a stronger balance sheet and support a solid investment grade rating.

The news comes days after the company parted ways with Chief Executive Officer Mads Nipper, who has been replaced by Rasmus Errboe, its former deputy chief executive and chief commercial officer.

On Thursday, Orsted reported fourth-quarter Ebitda excluding new partnerships and cancellation fees--the company's preferred metric--of 7.55 billion kroner, down from 8.62 billion kroner a year prior.

The net loss widened to 6.08 billion kroner from a loss of 284 million kroner, as revenue fell 2.1% to 21.08 billion kroner.

A FactSet consensus had seen net profit at 2.74 billion kroner on revenue of 26.02 billion kroner.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.description: Daikin Industries has an average rating of outperform and price targets ranging from 16,000 yen to 25,000 yen, according to analysts polled by Capital IQ.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
S&P 500 Turns Higher; Google, AMD in Focus

U.S. stocks closed with gains as earnings and economic data retook center stage in markets and political turbulence took a step back.

German Factory Orders Get Late-2024 Boost as Tariffs Loom

Manufacturing orders in Germany rose in December on aerospace demand, although any tentative signs of a recovery for the struggling industrial sector face the imminent threat of tariffs from the U.S.

Hawkish BOJ Board Member Calls for Potentially Faster Rate Hikes

In a speech to business leaders, Naoki Tamura said that the central bank should raise rates to 1% or higher in the fiscal half starting in October.

BOE Expected to Cut Interest Rates. Why That's Good for the Dollar.

Investors are pricing in a 97% chance that the U.K. central bank lowers its key rate to 4.5% from 4.75% on Thursday.

Trump and Musk's Actions Amount to Significant Pullback on Financial Regulation

Agencies overseeing consumer financial affairs, wealth managers, and commodity traders all seem to be scaling back oversight activity.

Fannie, Freddie Privatization, Cost-Cutting and New Name Are Priorities for HUD Chief

Scott Turner says he plans to quickly launch a review to root out inefficiencies at the agency.

Why Trump Is Closing a Trade Exemption for China

Here's how to understand de minimis and how it turbocharged bargain platforms Shein and Temu.

How DeepSeek could deliver another blow to the stock market

DeepSeek's full impact isn't yet reflected in U.S. stock prices, says Don Townswick at Conning

Dining Out Now Comes With a 50 Cent 'Egg Surcharge'

Waffle House and other restaurants are responding to a bird flu-related egg shortage with an added fee.

Fed's Barkin Says Economy Is Fine. Government Policy Is a Wild Card.

It's too early to determine the impact of tariffs and other Trump policies on the economy, but the uncertainty is having an effect, the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond president said.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
AstraZeneca Expects More Sales, Earnings Growth After Results Beat Market Views

The pharmaceuticals major was boosted by high demand for its key drugs that offset a slight sales drop in China.

Eli Lilly's Results Already Disappointed the Street. More Details Come Thursday.

The company disclosed preliminary results in mid January, but it left out important metrics such as earnings per share for the fourth quarter.

Tesla sales are diving in Germany and across Europe too

Tesla sales careened lower in Germany in January, as sales of the electric vehicle company headed by Elon Musk tumble across Europe.

OpenAI Set to Make Super Bowl Ad Debut

The TV spot marks the artificial-intelligence company's biggest foray into consumer advertising to date.

Allstate Expects California Wildfires to Cost $1.1 Billion

In the fourth quarter, catastrophe losses took $315 million out of Allstate's homeowners-insurance business, up from $21 million the year before.

Amazon Earnings Are Today. Why It's All About AWS.

Wall Street expects Amazon Web Servicesrevenue of $28.8 billion for the quarter, an increase from last year's $24.2 billion.

Carl's Jr. Returns to Racy Advertising Years After It Abandoned 'Bikinis and Burgers'

The burger chain dropped its sexualized campaigns in 2017. Now the company has returned to that approach with an "unapologetic, bold" social-media personality.

Sonos to Cut 12% of Jobs in Further Restructuring Push

The audio-products maker expects to book restructuring charges of $15 million to $18 million as it reorganizes.

Hillenbrand Sells Majority Stake in Milacron to Bain Capital Affiliate

The provider of highly-engineered processing equipment and solutions will sell a 51% stake in the injection molding and extrusion business for $287 million.

Salesforce's Longtime Operating Chief Millham to Retire; Washington Named Successor and Financial Chief

Operating Chief Brian Millham to exit after more than 25 at the company. Incoming CFO Robin Washington has served on Salesforce's board since 2013.

data.title: Kering's Gucci Parts Ways With Creative Director Sabato De Sarno
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

Kering's flagship label Gucci said it ended its collaboration with creative director Sabato De Sarno, at a time when the brand is facing weak demand and as the French group seeks to rejuvenate its fashion houses.

The fall-winter 2025 fashion show in Milan, which will take place on Feb. 25, will be presented by the Gucci design office, the company said Thursday. A new artistic direction will be announced in due time.

The move follows a number of changes at the French luxury giant led by Francois-Henri Pinault. In December, the group appointed a new creative director for its Bottega Veneta brand. It had previously named new chief executives at Saint Laurent and Balenciaga.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Corporate Actions Expert Switches Teams After 18-Year Fidelity Run
data.description:
Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE: BR) has appointed Larry Conover as Vice President and Special Advisor for Proxy & Corporate Actions, strengthening its leadership team with an experienced executive from Fidelity Investments.

Former-Fidelity Executive Joins Broadridge for Proxy Advisory Role

Conover joins the fintech leader after an 18-year tenure at Fidelity, where he served as Vice President for the Operations and Services Group. In his previous role, he managed the Asset Services Division's domestic and global corporate actions and proxy operations.

“I have been very fortunate to have a long and rewarding career at Fidelity that allowed me to grow both personally and professionally,” Conover commented. “It’s been an honor and privilege to be able to collaborate with such talented and dedicated professionals to advance the goals of the organization and create greater efficiencies to help customers.”

In his new role at Broadridge, based in New York, Conover will work on improving the company’s products and services while supporting its global clients. His responsibilities will also include efforts to streamline industry processes, such as implementing end-to-end confirmation for proxy contests.

This is another C-level move for the company, following the December appointment of Ashima Ghei as Chief Financial Officer (CFO). Ghei had previously served as interim CFO since July.

With over 25 years in financial services, Conover has participated in industry working groups and advisory committees, including SIFA Proxy Task Force. His work includes assessing regulatory and legislative changes and developing industry standards.

“My journey continues, as I am not quite done yet in the industry,” Conover concluded.

Broadridge Posts Strong Q2 Earnings

Last week, the company reported a robust second-quarter performance, with adjusted earnings per share surging 70% as the financial technology firm benefited from increased investor communication activity and steady recurring revenue growth.

Broadridge posted adjusted earnings of $1.56 per share for the quarter ended December 31, outpacing the previous year's $0.92. Net earnings more than doubled to $142 million, while total revenues climbed 13% to $1.59 billion.

The company's core recurring revenues grew 9% to $980 million. Event-driven revenues also saw a surge, jumping 126% to $125 million, primarily due to increased mutual fund communications activity.

Back in December, First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB) chose Broadridge Financial Solutions to support its global agency securities finance operations, expanding securities lending in the UAE and the wider Middle East. This collaboration aligns with Broadridge's introduction of an AI-driven algorithmic trading insights service

data.title: Telenor Swings to Net Profit, Boosted by Transformation Plan — Update
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Telenor swung to net profit in the fourth quarter, driven by its transformation strategy, despite booking lower revenue.

The Norwegian telecommunications provider posted Thursday a net profit of 1.735 billion Norwegian kroner ($154.6 million), in line with market consensus of 1.73 billion kroner based on eight analysts' estimates taken from FactSet. A year earlier, the company had booked a 7.2 billion-krone impairment of investment in True Corporation, resulting in a quarterly loss of 7.68 billion kroner.

Adjusted earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortization--the company's preferred measure--slightly increased to 8.48 billion kroner from 8.47 billion kroner, boosted by performance in the Nordics region.

Revenue fell to 20.50 billion kroner from 20.93 billion kroner, missing market consensus of 20.79 billion kroner, based on 11 analysts' forecasts taken from FactSet.

Meanwhile, service revenue increased 1.1% in the quarter to 16.27 billion kroner. In the Nordics, organic service revenue grew 2.3%, reflecting mainly growth in mobile service revenue and led by a 2.1% rise in Finland. However, in Asia, revenue decreased 3.3% due to a decline at its Grameenphone business. The challenging environment in Bangladesh was partially offset by growth at Telenor Pakistan, the company added.

The board declared a total dividend of 9.60 kroner a share, up from 9.50 kroner a share.

Looking ahead, Telenor expects low to mid-single-digit organic growth in Ebitda this year. For the Nordics, it is targeting mid-single-digit organic growth in Ebitda and low single-digit organic growth in service revenue. The company anticipates free cash flow of around 13 billion kroner before mergers and acquisitions and potential items.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: BBGI Global Infrastructure Agrees to $1.33 Billion Cash Takeover
data.description:
By Ian Walker

BBGI Global Infrastructure agreed to a 1.06 billion-pound ($1.33 billion) cash offer from Boswell Holdings 3, a new company indirectly controlled by British Columbia Investment Management.

The investment company said Thursday that accepting shareholders would get 147.5 pence in cash for each share held, a 21% premium to its closing price of 121.80 pence on Wednesday.

London-listed BBGI said it expects to declare its interim dividend for the half year ended Dec. 31 in February for payment in April. Given that the payment will be made before the deal is expected to complete, the offer price will be reduced by the value of the dividend.

BCI is a Canadian institutional investor with 250 billion Canadian dollars ($174.68 billion) in gross assets under management at March 31, 2024. It manages a portfolio of diversified public and private market investments on behalf of its British Columbia public pension fund and institutional clients.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Buy Godrej Properties; target of Rs 3435: Motilal Oswal
data.description:
Motilal Oswal's research report on Godrej Properties

GPL’s pre-sales volume for 3QFY25 declined 6% YoY (-21% QoQ) to 4.1msf, resulting in a pre-sales value of INR54.5b (-5% YoY/5% QoQ). Notably, 77% of the contribution came from newly launched projects. For 9MFY25, presales were up 48% YoY to INR193b. In 3QFY25, GPL launched seven projects across four cities, with a total cumulative saleable potential of 2.2msf, and delivered 2.6msf.

Outlook

We reiterate our BUY rating with a revised TP of INR3,435 (previously INR3,724), implying a 44% potential upside. The reduction in TP is due to dilution as a result of the QIP issue.

data.title: Buy Kajaria Ceramics; target of Rs 1120: Motilal Oswal
data.description:
Motilal Oswal's research report on Kajaria Ceramics

Kajaria Ceramics (KJC)’s 3QFY25 EBITDA was below our estimate due to lowerthan- estimated revenue and weak realization. EBITDA declined ~17% YoY to INR1.5b (14% miss) and OPM contracted 2.7pp YoY to ~13% (est. ~14%). Adj. PAT fell ~25% YoY to INR777m (25% miss), dragged down by lower-thanestimated other income and higher-than-estimated interest costs. Management highlighted that weakness in the domestic market and sluggish exports led to another subdued quarter for the tile industry. It anticipates volume growth of ~8-9% YoY in FY25, lower than its initial double-digit growth guidance. Plywood revenue declined significantly due to higher raw material prices, and KJC is now waiting for demand recovery rather than pushing sales (focusing on retail business).

Outlook

Valuation at 37x/32x FY26/27E EPS appears reasonable; though, key trigger for stock price performance would be the demand recovery. We maintain our BUY rating with a revised TP of INR1,120 (earlier INR1,450), based on 40x Dec’26E EPS.

For all recommendations report, click here

data.title: Neutral Eris Lifesciences; target of Rs 1270: Motilal Oswal
data.description:
Motilal Oswal's research report on Eris Lifesciences

Eris Lifescience’s (ERIS) 3QFY25 performance came in below our estimates. Lower-than-expected revenue growth led to lower operating leverage, which affected the overall performance. The base business witnessed some recovery in growth owing to new launches and price hikes. We reduce our FY25 EPS estimate by 4% (factoring in lower sales from biologics business) and largely maintain our estimates for FY26/FY27. We value ERIS at 25x 12M forward earnings to arrive at a TP of INR1,270. ERIS is building the GLP1 franchise by ensuring the sourcing of API for synthetic peptide and subsequently conducting clinical trials/enhancing manufacturing capacity for recombinant semaglutide. It continues to launch combination products in the SGLT2 space. Considering this and a reduction in financial leverage, we estimate a CAGR of 16%/42% in EBITDA/earnings over FY25-27. However, the current valuation leaves limited upside. Reiterate Neutral.

Outlook

We reduce our FY25 EPS estimate by 4% (factoring lower sales from biologics business) and largely maintain our estimates for FY26/FY27. We value ERIS at 25x 12M forward earnings to arrive at a TP of INR1,270.

data.title: Buy Tata Power Company; target of Rs 485: Sharekhan
data.description:
Sharekhan's research report on Tata Power Company

Consolidated Adj. PAT of Rs. 1,031 crore was up 8.2% y-o-y led by Mundra UMPP benefit (Rs. 332 crore), good performance in the renewable business and lower tax rate. Standalone PAT increased 86% y-o-y, Solar EPC PAT rose 50% y-o-y and the 4.3 GW cell & module plant added to the profitability. Company intends to add 4-5GW of renewable capacities in the next two years.

Outlook

Tata Power has a well-planned strategy to shift towards clean energy and targets for 2.5x rise in its PAT by FY2030E over FY2024. We maintain a Buy on Tata Power with a revised PT of Rs. 485 on a SOTP basis

data.title: Buy Info Edge India; target of Rs 9100: Sharekhan
data.description:
Sharekhan's research report on Info Edge India

Info Edge’s standalone revenue stood at Rs. 672 crore, up 12.8% y-o-y, slightly missing our estimate of Rs. 682 crore. EBITDA margin expanded by 130 bps q-o-q/270 bps y-o-y to 43.1%, beating our estimate of 41%. The recruitment business is demonstrating sustained growth across segments, driven by strengthened go-to-market efforts, expansion into new client segments, and strong performance from niche businesses.

Outlook

We roll forward to FY27E and maintain BUY on Info Edge with SOTP-based revised PT of Rs. 9,100. At CMP, the stock trades at 71.7x FY27E EPS and 58.2x FY27E EV/EBITDA.

data.title: Dollar Rises Amid Uncertainty Over U.S. Trade Policy — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar edges higher as uncertainty around President Trump's trade policy remains elevated. Safe-haven demand should persist for the time being, with market participants seeking shelter in the dollar as a result of ongoing policy uncertainties, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Trump's tariff plans will have a less detrimental impact on the U.S. economy compared to developed market peers, he says. This effectively puts the dollar in a position of being "the best of a bad bunch." The DXY dollar index rises 0.2% to 107.768 after hitting a one-week low of 107.296 Wednesday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Huawei Revenue Jumped 22% in 2024, Chairman Says
data.description:
Huawei's annual revenue topped 860 billion yuan in 2024, up 22% from 704.2 billion yuan in 2023, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing Chairman Liang Hua's remarks at a forum in Guangzhou, China.

The tech firm's overall operations are seen to "meet expectations" while ICT infrastructure will remain steady while its consumer operations will return to growth, the report said, citing Liang.

Huawei's share in the Chinese smartphone market reached 16% in 2024, Reuters reported, citing data from Canalys.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Zhejiang Tiancheng Controls Receives 1.6 Million Yuan in Government Subsidies
data.description:
Zhejiang Tiancheng Controls received 1.6 million yuan in government subsidies.

The amount accounts for 11% of the automobile seat producer's 2023 attributable net profit, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of Zhejiang Tiancheng Controls rose more than 3% Thursday.

data.title: Triveni Engineering Ties Up With Rolls-Royce Marine for 4MW Marine Gas Turbine Generators
data.description:
Triveni Engineering & Industries signed a memorandum of understanding with Rolls-Royce Marine North America to collaborate on programs for 4MW marine gas turbine generators (GTG) in India, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian bourses.

The two companies will collaborate on designing, developing, and manufacturing the marine GTGs, as well as on sales and support activities.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Adnoc Gas Net Profit Beats Market Expectations
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Adnoc Gas, one of the world's biggest natural-gas producers, reported fourth-quarter net income that beat market expectations, and said it expects capital expenditure to rise this year.

The Abu Dhabi-owned gas company on Thursday said net profit for the quarter rose to $1.38 billion from $1.35 billion it reported for the same period a year earlier, beating the $1.03 billion analysts polled by Visible Alpha had expected.

The company said lower volumes hurt its result, although this was partially offset by better domestic margins.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose 3% on year to $2.28 billion.

Fourth-quarter sales volumes were 869 trillion British thermal units, down 5% on the same period a year earlier, hit by planned shutdowns.

For 2025, Adnoc said it expects capital expenditure to rise to between $3.0 billion and $3.5 billion from $1.84 billion last year due to higher spending on its ethane recovery and monetization project and maintenance across its business.

It expects domestic gas production for the year of between 2,320 trillion and 2,390 trillion British thermal units compared with the 2,330 trillion BTU it produced in 2024.

For the first quarter, Adnoc said it expects a decline in net income from the previous quarter due to production shutdowns at liquefied natural gas and other sites which will have an impact of at least $100 million.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Bharat Dynamics Clocks Gains in Fiscal Q3 Profit
data.description:
Bharat Dynamics recorded a rise in its profit to 1.47 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 1.35 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share came in at 4.01 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 3.68 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the defense equipment manufacturer.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 also increased to 8.32 billion rupees from 6.02 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board declared an interim dividend of 4 rupees per share of the face value of 5 rupees each for the fiscal year ending March 31.

Shares of the company were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Trade War Between U.S. and China Could Weaken Eurozone Economy, Says ECB's Cipollone
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

A trade war between the U.S. and China could lead to weaker economic growth and falling inflation in the eurozone, a top official at the European Central Bank warned in an interview published Thursday.

Speaking to Reuters, ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone said high U.S. tariffs on imports from the world's second largest economy could push Chinese businesses to look for buyers elsewhere, and increase their competition with eurozone rivals.

"Trade barriers will force China to sell its goods elsewhere and the competition from China could be a serious threat to us," he said. "These goods showing up in Europe could have both a deflationary and a contractionary impact because they would crowd out local products."

The U.S. and China entered a new trade war on Tuesday, with tariffs of 10% going into effect on Chinese goods. Beijing struck back with 10%-15% levies that will take effect Feb. 10 on select American goods.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: A.P. Moller-Maersk Swings to Profit as Red Sea Disruptions Boost Freight Rates — Update
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

A.P. Moeller-Maersk swung to a better-than-expected net profit in the fourth quarter as disruptions to the Red Sea shipping supported higher shipping rates.

The Danish shipping company said it made $2.085 billion in net profit for the quarter, compared with a net loss of $436 million a year ago, when an oversupply of cargo capacity weighed on freight rates.

Quarterly revenue came to $14.59 billion, a 24% on-year increase as continued disruption to shipping lanes in the Red Sea pushed up shipping rates by 38% in the quarter.

The result compares with analysts' expectations of $1.31 billion in net profit on $13.89 billion in revenue, according to a Visible Alpha consensus.

Maersk's ocean segment, its container shipping business and the company's largest division, posted a 39% increase to revenue in the reporting period.

The company said it would propose a 1,120 Danish kroner dividend after separately announcing Wednesday evening the start of a share buyback program of up to around $2 billion.

The company said that for 2025 it is targeting $6 billion to $9 billion in underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, up to $3 billion in underlying earnings before interest and taxes.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Trade War Between U.S. And China Could Weaken Eurozone Economy, ECB's Cipollone Says — Update
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

A trade war between the U.S. and China could lead to weaker economic growth and falling inflation in the eurozone, a top official at the European Central Bank warned in an interview published Thursday.

Speaking to Reuters, ECB Executive Board member Piero Cipollone said high U.S. tariffs on imports from the world's second largest economy could push Chinese businesses to look for buyers elsewhere, and increase their competition with eurozone rivals.

"Trade barriers will force China to sell its goods elsewhere and the competition from China could be a serious threat to us," he said. "These goods showing up in Europe could have both a deflationary and a contractionary impact because they would crowd out local products."

The U.S. and China entered a new trade war on Tuesday, with tariffs of 10% going into effect on Chinese goods. Beijing struck back with 10%-15% levies that are set to take effect on Feb. 10 on select American goods.

The ECB last week lowered its key interest for a fifth time since June 2024, and investors expect further cuts in upcoming meetings of its governing council as inflation cools and economic growth remains weak.

However, Cipollone said that given the high levels of uncertainty about the economic outlook that partly derives from U.S. trade policy, "anything can happen."

"We all agree there is still room for adjusting rates downwards," he said. "But we need to be extremely careful."

U.S. President Trump has made it clear that U.S. allies across the Atlantic are high on his target list for tariffs. But Cipollone said the imbalances in trade between the two economic giants are smaller than they appear.

"It's true we have a goods surplus with the United States," he said. "But if you add in services and look at the overall current account, then the balance is close to zero."

ECB rate setters have become more concerned about the outlook for growth in the eurozone economy, which stagnated in the final quarter of last year.

Cipollone said that disappointing outcome might be a reflection of uncertainty about the outlook for the global economy in the run-up to the U.S. presidential election and its aftermath.

"The real problem is investments, and that is only partially linked to monetary policy," he said. "The culprit is uncertainty. Investments have been weak since the summer given the overall uncertainty and the direction of trade policy after the US election."

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: Anglo American Cuts 2025 Diamond View After Production Falls
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Anglo American reported a fall in fourth-quarter copper and diamond production and cut its 2025 diamond production guidance.

The London-listed mining giant said Thursday that it dug out 198,000 tons of copper, 14% less than in the fourth quarter of 2023, due to the planned shut down of its smaller Los Bronces plant.

Diamond production also fell 26% on year to 5.8 million carats from 7.9 million carats as Anglo American cut production in response to lower demand and higher than normal levels of inventory.

Anglo American also cuts its full-year 2025 diamond production guidance to between 20 million to 23 million carats, down from previous guidance of 30 million to 33 million carats.

The company kept its full-year guidance for copper production for 2025 and 2026 unchanged.

Across the business, all division met their full-year production guidance, the company said.

Anglo American also said it is making "excellent" progress with its portfolio simplification. The sales process for its nickel business is well progressed, and its continue to prepare the De Beers business for separation, the company said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Jingxi Culture & Tourism Logs 1.01 Billion Yuan in Box Office Revenues
data.description:
Beijing Jingxi Culture & Tourism generated 1.01 billion yuan in box office revenues after the release of the movie The Second Part of the Conferred God: War in Xiqi on Jan. 29, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Wednesday.

The figures exceeded 50% of the operating income of the company's audited consolidated financial statements for the most recent fiscal year.

The Chinese tour operator's operating income from the film is expected to be between 90 million yuan and 120 million yuan as of Tuesday, Feb. 4, the disclosure said.

The company's shares dropped over 6% at the close.
